mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2 percentage point threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand growth across goods and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boost corporate earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with INDPRO levels over any 24-month rolling period, with a hit rate below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 245, persistent price pressures force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in greater than 70 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 245 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or insignificant predictive coefficient for 20-day returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive directional bias when GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0 percent, with a directional accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production alongside falling personal consumption expenditures signal a synchronized supply-demand contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases where both INDPRO and RSAFS decline simultaneously over a 3-year window.
